missionOverview.title: NASA's Psyche Mission
missionOverview.description: Psyche is a NASA mission to explore a unique metal-rich asteroid, also named Psyche, orbiting the Sun between Mars and Jupiter. The mission aims to help scientists understand the building blocks of planet formation by studying a body that may be the exposed core of an early planet.

funFacts.fact: Asteroid 16 Psyche is one of the most massive objects in the main asteroid belt, with an estimated diameter of about 140 miles (226 km),roughly the size of Massachusetts.
Unlike most asteroids that are rocky or icy, Psyche appears to be largely made of iron and nickel,similar to Earth's core.
Scientists believe Psyche may be the exposed metallic core of a protoplanet that lost its rocky outer layers due to violent collisions billions of years ago.
Psyche orbits the Sun between Mars and Jupiter at a distance ranging from 235 to 309 million miles (378 to 497 million km) from the Sun.
If Psyche's metal could be brought to Earth, it would be worth an estimated $10,000 quadrillion,far more than the entire world economy.
A day on Psyche lasts just over 4 hours. The asteroid completes one full rotation on its axis in about 4.196 hours.
Psyche was discovered in 1852 by Italian astronomer Annibale de Gasparis and was named after the Greek goddess of the soul.
The Psyche spacecraft is about the size of a tennis court with its solar panels deployed, measuring roughly 81 feet (24.76 m) long and 24 feet (7.34 m) wide.
Psyche is the first NASA deep-space mission to use Hall-effect thrusters for propulsion. These super-efficient electric engines use xenon gas and electromagnetic fields to generate thrust.
The spacecraft's solar electric propulsion system produces a gentle glow of blue ionized xenon gas, creating a sci-fi-worthy blue exhaust trail.

Despite using advanced thrusters, the maximum thrust is only about the force you'd feel holding three quarters in your hand,but in the vacuum of space, that's enough to reach incredible speeds over time.
The spacecraft carries about 2,030 pounds (922 kg) of xenon propellant in its two tanks,enough to fill a small car's trunk.
Psyche launched on October 13, 2023, aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Kennedy Space Center in Florida.
The spacecraft will travel roughly 2.2 billion miles (3.6 billion km) through the solar system before arriving at asteroid Psyche in August 2029.
Psyche performed a Mars gravity assist flyby in May 2026, using Mars's gravitational pull to slingshot toward the asteroid belt.
The mission is led by Arizona State University. NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory manages the mission and built the spacecraft.
Psyche is the 14th mission selected as part of NASA's Discovery Program, which supports lower-cost, highly focused science investigations.
We can never visit Earth's own core,it lies about 1,800 miles beneath our feet under extreme pressure and heat. Psyche gives us a chance to study a planetary core directly.
Understanding Psyche could teach us how rocky planets like Earth, Mars, and Mercury formed and differentiated into layers of crust, mantle, and core.

The Psyche mission will help answer a fundamental question: what happens during the most violent chapters of a planetary system's early history, when planetary bodies collide and lose their outer layers?
